Reshape the group and drive growth

Privatizing Hang Seng Bank - On January 26, 2026, after approval by shareholders and the court, We completed the privatization of Hang Seng Bank. Hang Seng Bank is currently A wholly-owned subsidiary of the HSBC Group, its shares are already in Hong Kong The Stock Exchange revokes its listing status. This transaction reflection set The group is confident in Hong Kong's prospects and further consolidates the Group's market leadership position. - By privatizing Hang Seng Bank, we expect two Hong Kong products Brand could achieve more than $500 million before tax by the end of 2028 Revenue and cost synergies, related restructuring costs to US$600 million. These costs are reported as significant and require attention project. We plan to focus on savings from cost synergies The new allocation to HSBC provides a range of competitive advantages and value-added returns. domain. - Our ambition also includes having two Hong Kong brand further generates approximately US$400 million in revenue and costs opportunity.

Simplify organizational structure - In the 2024 full-year results, we announced the simplification of the group structure agency measures, committing to reduce the cost base by approximately US$1.5 billion. Annualize costs and plan to simplify organizational structure to predict It is planned to achieve the goal by the end of 2026.

Progress in increasing scope - In terms of wealth management business, we are moving towards wealth management Focus on business investment and hire more account managers. in 2025 As of December 31, 2019, the wealth management industry of each business segment of the Group The financial balance reached US$2.1 trillion, an increase from the same period last year 16%. Among them, the net new investment assets we attracted reached US$80 billion, of which US$39 billion was accounted for in Asia. In comparison, the net new investment assets in 2024 will be US$64 billion, of which US$47 billion was accounted for in Asia. - Transaction banking continues to perform well and we take full advantage own networks and capabilities to understand changes in trade and capital flows opportunities brought about by change. Founded in 1865, HSBC is one of the world's largest banking and financial services institutions.
We use our unique expertise, capabilities, understanding and vision to help our clients explore global opportunities.

Group strategy The Group's strategy supports our ambition to become the world's most trustworthy bank by always putting customers first and service first. We have a huge International network, stable and strong asset and liability strength, and through the provision of group products and services, we are committed to meeting the financial needs of customers and supporting them to achieve their goals Goal, galloping in the complex and ever-changing global market.

Our goal is to make decisions quickly and stably Appropriate, keep up with trends, adapt to changes, promote Simplify the structure and use technology and digitalization technology to prepare for the future.

We are highly customer-oriented and help our customers win Achieve outstanding results and strive to increase customer loyalty degree, and is involved in various businesses of the group in many areas, providing services to customers in the long run service.
As a premier international bank, we Committed to promoting long-term sustainable growth, paying equal attention to Identify areas of competitive advantage.
From January 1, 2025, the HSBC Group's operations will consist of four new businesses to simplify the organizational structure and accelerate the implementation of priority strategies.
1 Calculated based on revenue by business segment, excluding Corporate Centers.

The Hong Kong business has a market leading position, including the Hong Kong HSBC and Hang Seng Bank's retail banking and wealth management industries services, as well as industrial and commercial financial services.

The UK business has market leadership positions, including Zero Sales of banking and wealth management businesses (including first direct and M&S Bank), as well as UK industrial and commercial finance business (including including HSBC Innovation Bank).
International Wealth Management and Financial Excellence

Corporate and institutional financial management business is a cross-border transaction bank and capital Market leaders in the two fields of marketing business, among which the United Kingdom industrial and commercial financial services outside China and Hong Kong, as well as global banking banking and capital markets business.

International wealth management and financial excellence businesses include Hong Kong and Excellence in wealth management and private banking outside the UK services, investment management business and insurance business.

HSBC's values guide all the Group's actions, from strategic decisions to daily interactions with customers and colleagues. Our values are rooted in HSBC's history, heritage and corporate culture The foundation helps us implement the group's purpose.
